channel.title: HPCC Service Status
channel.description: We'll post information about ICER's system downtimes, updates, new features, and other information for the ICER user community here.

item.title: Gateway and Development node updates
item.description: Beginning Wednesday, April 15, 2026, we will be performing minor security updates to the gateway and development nodes. A Wall message will be posted to the node advising of upcoming reboots at least one (1) hour before the system is unavailable due to the reboot. Multiple reboots are possible, but will take place in rapid succession if needed. No impact to functionality after the upgrades is expected, but if you encounter any issues or have any questions, please contact us. An update will be posted when updates are complete.

item.title: Development Node Configuration Change
item.description: On Tuesday, April 14th, at 8:00PM, we will be applying a configuration change to the development nodes in order to increase system stability. This change will reserve a portion of memory solely for system processes. This will mitigate situations where high memory demand could otherwise cause development nodes to become unresponsive. This configuration change is expected to be non-disruptive, however, if you encounter any issues or have any questions, please contact us

item.title: HPCC Scheduled Maintenance
item.description:
The HPCC will be unavailable on Thursday, May 7 starting at 5AM for regularly scheduled maintenance. No jobs will run during this time.

Jobs that will not be completed before this date will not begin until after maintenance is complete. For example, if you submit a four day job three days before the maintenance outage, your job will be postponed and will not begin to run until after maintenance is completed.

If you have any questions, please contact us

item.title: Network Maintenance 4/9/2026
item.description: On Thursday, April 9, 2026, between 9AM and 11AM we will be installing two new network switches. No impact to running jobs is expected. If you notice any issues with connectivity or running jobs between 9AM and 11AM please open a ticket.

item.title: Research File System Quota Troubleshooting Beginning 4/9/2026
item.description: Beginning Thursday, April 9, 2026, we will be working with our vendor to troubleshoot a technical issue with the Research Space quota system. No outage is anticipated; however, you may notice periodic slowdowns while we troubleshoot this issue. This message will be updated as troubleshooting continues, and will be resolved once the quota system is returned to normal operation. If you experience any issues accessing a Research Space or Project directory during this time, please open a ticket.

item.title: Operating System Updates Beginning 4/1/2026
item.description: Over the next several weeks, ICER will be performing minor operating system updates across all nodes in the HPCC. During this time, users may notice longer queue times and some nodes unavailable in Slurm while they are being updated. Once the updates are complete, nodes will be returned to service and jobs will continue to schedule and run. Because these are minor updates, the software and module system is unaffected. No issues have been identified in initial testing, but please open a ticket if you experience any issues.

item.title: Legacy Software Module Maintenance Tuesday 03/31/26
item.description: On Tuesday, 31 March 2026 we will be changing the backend for legacy (CentOS 7) versions of our software modules. This will require remounting the legacy software modules across the cluster. If you are using the legacy CentOS-7 container to run workloads on this date, you may see an interruption of your work.

item.title: Scheduler Reboot at 8:00PM on 3/9/26 - RESOLVED
item.description:
RESOLVED 8:02PM: The scheduling server is back online.

At 8:00PM on Monday, March 9th, the SLURM scheduling server will go offline for a reboot. This reboot is necessary to increase the host’s memory and improve stability. The scheduler is expected to be offline for less than 15 minutes. During this time, jobs may not be submitted and scheduler specific client commands will not work (e.g. squeue, sbatch, etc). Running jobs will not be affected. If you have any questions about this outage, please contact us.

item.title: Home Filesystem Maintenance Friday 03/06/26 - RESOLVED
item.description:
RESOLVED: 3/6/2026 - The home file system has been successfully upgraded.

On Friday, March 6th, at 7AM, an update will be applied to the home file system. No outage or performance impacts are anticipated. This blog will be updated once the upgrade is complete.

item.title: Request Tracker (RT) Upgrade -- RESOLVED
item.description:
Resolved 2/27/2026: The ticketing system has been updated and is back online.

Request Tracker (rt.hpcc.msu.edu) will be unavailable starting 7am on Tues March 3rd until 12pm. During that time no tickets can be created or referenced. The Contact Form will be unavailable too. We are migrating to a newer major version. We will provide updates here as needed.
